body:value: The requirement of infrastructure is calculated using the prescribed norms on the basis of provisional rural population from Census, 2011 and estimated rural tribal population in 2011 using percentage of tribal population out of rural population in 2001. All India shortfall is derived by adding state-wise figures of shortfall ignoring the existing surplus in some of the states.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Health Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: Shortfall in Health Infrastructure

body:value: The Sub Divisional and District Hospitals are government hospitals with more than 30 beds. Mobile Medical Units have been envisaged under National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) to provide preventive, promotive and curative health care in inaccessible areas and difficult terrains, which are underserved or unserved areas under usual circumstances. Functional Mobile Medical Units not only look after the curative aspects but also render Behaviour Change Communication (BCC) activities to promote healthy under the NRHM.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Health Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: Sub Divisional Hospitals, District Hospitals and Mobile Medical Units Functioning

body:value: Get details on total budget and expenditure of State Agricultural Universities (SAUs) in India. The data set includes the fund allocation and expenditure of the SAUs from their respective states, ICAR and other sources. Total fund allocation and expenditure is also given in the data set. The state-wise details of the fund allocation and expenditure is given from year 2004-05 till 2012-13 for the state (data for all the years is not available for all the states).
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE)
Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)
field_sector:name: Agriculture
Education
Higher Education
title: Total budget and expenditure of State Agricultural Universities

body:value: The data refers to sector-wise funds proposed in District Perspective Plans (DPPs) and funds actually utilized in the five districts, namely, Warrangal (Andhra Pradesh), Pallakad (Kerala), Mayurbhanj (Orrissa), Mon (Nagaland) and Dangs (Gujarat). There were very trivial or no deviations in the amount proposed in the DPPs for various interventions and the amount actually spent on them.

Infrastructure

Employment
